summary.title: Gin & Juice Shop - Threat Model
summary.description: Comprehensive STRIDE threat model for https://ginandjuice.shop - a deliberately vulnerable e-commerce web application by PortSwigger. This model covers all major attack surfaces including authentication, product catalog, shopping cart, blog, newsletter subscription, and coupon system.

diagrams.title: Gin & Juice Shop - Data Flow Diagram (Main Architecture)

data.name: Anonymous User
data.description: Unauthenticated visitor browsing the shop, viewing products, blog posts, and subscribing to newsletter.

threats.title: Attacker impersonates legitimate user session
threats.description: Attacker can steal session cookies via XSS or network sniffing and impersonate a logged-in user.
threats.mitigation: Use HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookie flags. Implement HTTPS everywhere. Use Content Security Policy.

data.name: Authenticated User
data.description: Logged-in customer who can view account details, purchase products, and manage cart.

threats.title: Horizontal privilege escalation between user accounts
threats.description: User may access another user's account data by manipulating account ID parameters (e.g., /my-account?id=2).
threats.mitigation: Implement server-side authorization checks. Never trust client-supplied user IDs.

data.name: Attacker
data.description: Malicious actor attempting to exploit vulnerabilities in the application.

data.name: Vulnerability Scanner
data.description: Automated web vulnerability scanner (e.g., Burp Suite) used to test the application.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Intentional use case - app is designed for scanner testing

data.name: Web Frontend
data.description: Client-side rendering of product pages, blog, cart, and account pages. Served over HTTPS.

threats.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via product/blog content
threats.description: Unsanitized user-generated content (blog comments, product search) may be reflected or stored and executed as JavaScript in victim browsers.
threats.mitigation: Implement output encoding. Use Content-Security-Policy headers. Sanitize all user inputs before rendering.

threats.title: JavaScript source code reveals internal API endpoints
threats.description: Client-side JavaScript may expose internal API paths, parameter names, or business logic.
threats.mitigation: Minify and obfuscate production JS. Avoid embedding sensitive configuration in frontend code.

data.name: Authentication Service
data.description: Handles user login at /login endpoint. Username: carlos. Manages session cookies.

threats.title: Username enumeration via login response differences
threats.description: Different error messages for invalid username vs. invalid password allow attackers to enumerate valid usernames.
threats.mitigation: Return identical error messages and response times for all failed login attempts.

threats.title: Brute force attack on login endpoint
threats.description: No rate limiting on /login allows automated password guessing. Default user 'carlos' is publicly known.
threats.mitigation: Implement account lockout, CAPTCHA, or exponential backoff. Add rate limiting per IP and per account.

threats.title: SQL Injection in login form credentials
threats.description: Username or password fields may be vulnerable to SQL injection, allowing authentication bypass.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements. Implement input validation.

threats.title: Insufficient authentication event logging
threats.description: Failed and successful login attempts may not be logged with sufficient detail for forensic analysis.
threats.mitigation: Log all authentication events with timestamp, IP, user agent. Alert on anomalous patterns.

data.name: Product Catalog
data.description: Serves product listings and details at /catalog and /catalog/product?productId=N. Handles search functionality.

threats.title: SQL Injection via productId parameter
threats.description: The productId parameter in /catalog/product?productId= is likely vulnerable to SQL injection (product name 'Original Dry SQLi Cocktail' hints at this). Attacker can dump the database.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries. Validate that productId is a positive integer. Implement WAF rules.

threats.title: SQL Injection via product search functionality
threats.description: The search input in /catalog may pass unsanitized input to SQL queries.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries. Implement input length limits and character whitelist.

threats.title: Verbose error messages expose database structure
threats.description: SQL errors may be returned directly to the client, revealing table names, column names, or DB version.
threats.mitigation: Implement custom error pages. Log errors server-side. Never expose stack traces to users.

threats.title: Resource exhaustion via unbounded search queries
threats.description: Automated or malicious search queries with no rate limiting can exhaust server resources.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting, query timeouts, and pagination limits.

data.name: Shopping Cart
data.description: Manages cart state at /catalog/cart. Handles add/remove items and checkout process.

threats.title: Price manipulation via cart parameter tampering
threats.description: Client-side price values submitted in cart POST requests may not be validated server-side, allowing price manipulation.
threats.mitigation: Always calculate prices server-side using product IDs. Never trust client-submitted prices.

threats.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) on cart operations
threats.description: Cart add/remove/checkout operations may lack CSRF protection, allowing attacker-controlled sites to trigger actions.
threats.mitigation: Implement CSRF tokens on all state-changing operations. Use SameSite cookie attribute.

threats.title: Access other users' cart data
threats.description: Cart session IDs may be predictable or cart data may be accessible by manipulating session/cart identifiers.
threats.mitigation: Use cryptographically random session identifiers. Validate cart ownership server-side.

data.name: Blog
data.description: Blog at /blog and /blog/post?postId=N. Displays posts and may support comments.

threats.title: SQL Injection via postId parameter
threats.description: The postId parameter in /blog/post?postId= may be vulnerable to SQL injection allowing data exfiltration.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries. Validate postId as positive integer.

threats.title: Stored XSS via blog comments
threats.description: If the blog supports comments, unsanitized comment content stored in the database can execute scripts in other users' browsers.
threats.mitigation: Sanitize and encode all user-generated content. Implement CSP. Use HTML entity encoding.

threats.title: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in blog search
threats.description: Blog search functionality may pass user input directly into server-side template engines.
threats.mitigation: Never pass user input to template engines. Use logic-less templates or strict sandboxing.

data.name: Newsletter Subscription
data.description: Email subscription form present on multiple pages. Collects email addresses.

threats.title: Email header injection via subscription form
threats.description: Email field in subscription form may not sanitize CRLF characters, allowing email header injection for spam relay.
threats.mitigation: Validate email format strictly. Strip CR/LF characters. Use parameterized email libraries.

threats.title: Email bombing via subscription form abuse
threats.description: No CAPTCHA or rate limiting on subscription form allows bulk subscription requests, flooding mail infrastructure.
threats.mitigation: Add CAPTCHA or double opt-in. Implement rate limiting per IP.

threats.title: Subscriber email harvesting
threats.description: If subscriber confirmation is reflected in response, it may confirm email validity for attackers.
threats.mitigation: Use consistent responses regardless of whether email is already subscribed.

data.name: Coupon System
data.description: Handles coupon validation and application to shopping cart. '20% off everything' coupon advertised on site.

threats.title: Coupon code brute force / enumeration
threats.description: No rate limiting on coupon validation allows attackers to brute-force valid coupon codes.
threats.mitigation: Rate limit coupon validation attempts. Use cryptographically random coupon codes.

threats.title: Coupon reuse / stack discount attack
threats.description: Coupons may be applicable multiple times or stackable, allowing unintended discount accumulation.
threats.mitigation: Track coupon usage per user/order. Enforce single-use constraints server-side.

data.name: Image Service
data.description: Serves product and blog images from /image/scanme/ path. May process dynamic image requests.

threats.title: Path traversal in image serving endpoint
threats.description: The /image/scanme/ endpoint may be vulnerable to path traversal attacks (e.g., ../../etc/passwd) to read arbitrary files.
threats.mitigation: Validate and canonicalize file paths. Use allowlist of permitted directories. Run image service with minimal permissions.

threats.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via image URL parameter
threats.description: If image service accepts URLs as parameters, attacker can make the server fetch internal resources.
threats.mitigation: Use allowlist for permitted image sources. Block internal IP ranges. Disable URL-based image fetching.

threats.title: Image processing DoS via malformed files
threats.description: Malformed image files (image bombs) submitted to processing endpoints can exhaust CPU/memory.
threats.mitigation: Implement file size limits, processing timeouts, and image format validation.

data.name: Application Database
data.description: Backend database storing products, user accounts (carlos), blog posts, orders, coupons, and subscriber emails.

threats.title: Plaintext password storage
threats.description: User passwords (including default user 'carlos') may be stored in plaintext or with weak hashing.
threats.mitigation: Store passwords using bcrypt/scrypt/Argon2 with appropriate work factors. Never store plaintext.

threats.title: SQL Injection leading to data manipulation
threats.description: SQL injection vulnerabilities across multiple endpoints can allow attackers to INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DROP database objects.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries everywhere. Implement least-privilege database accounts.

threats.title: PII data exposure via SQL injection
threats.description: Database contains PII (user emails, names, addresses). SQL injection can expose all stored PII.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt PII at rest. Implement column-level encryption for sensitive fields. Regular security audits.

threats.title: Database resource exhaustion via complex queries
threats.description: Injected time-based or heavy queries (e.g., SLEEP(), cartesian joins) can exhaust database resources.
threats.mitigation: Set query timeouts. Monitor for long-running queries. Implement connection pooling limits.

data.name: Session Store
data.description: Stores user session data including authentication tokens and cart state.

threats.title: Session token predictability
threats.description: Session tokens may be generated with weak randomness, allowing prediction or brute-forcing of valid sessions.
threats.mitigation: Use cryptographically secure random session token generation (e.g., 128-bit entropy minimum).

threats.title: Session fixation attack
threats.description: Application may not regenerate session ID after login, allowing session fixation attacks.
threats.mitigation: Always regenerate session ID upon authentication. Invalidate old session on login.

data.name: Static File Store
data.description: Serves static resources including CSS, JS, images from /resources/ path.

threats.title: Directory listing exposes file structure
threats.description: Web server directory listing may be enabled, exposing file names and directory structure.
threats.mitigation: Disable directory listing. Ensure no sensitive files in public directories.

data.name: User HTTP Requests
data.description: HTTP/HTTPS requests from anonymous users to the web frontend. Includes page loads, form submissions.

threats.title: Sensitive data transmitted over HTTP (non-HTTPS)
threats.description: If any pages are served over HTTP instead of HTTPS, credentials and session tokens are exposed.
threats.mitigation: Enforce HTTPS everywhere. Implement HSTS headers. Redirect all HTTP to HTTPS.

threats.title: Man-in-the-Middle attack on unencrypted traffic
threats.description: Without enforced HTTPS, attacker on the network can intercept and modify traffic.
threats.mitigation: Enable HSTS with long max-age and includeSubDomains. Use HTTPS certificate pinning.

data.name: Authentication Request
data.description: POST /login with username and password credentials.

data.name: Database Queries
data.description: SQL queries from application processes to the backend database.

threats.title: Unsanitized SQL queries enable injection
threats.description: Application passes user input directly into SQL strings, enabling SQL injection attacks.
threats.mitigation: Use ORM or parameterized queries for all database interactions.
